g.data-narration: Decentralization using Blockchain explained for CSE students.
Decentralization distributes authority across a network instead of central control.
g.text: Decentralization is the distribution of authority and control across a network
rather than concentrating it in a single central entity. It enables peer-to-peer
interactions without intermediaries, enhancing security, transparency, and resilience.

g.data-narration: Compare centralized systems with single points of failure to decentralized networks.
Blockchain provides three pillars: consensus, P2P network, and cryptography.
g.text: Blockchain provides three key pillars for decentralization:

g.data-narration: Learn about Proof of Work, Proof of Stake, DPoS, and PBFT consensus algorithms.
g.text: Consensus algorithms ensure all nodes agree on a single version of truth:

g.data-narration: P2P networks enable equal participation where all nodes can validate and store the ledger.
g.text: In P2P networks, all nodes are equal participants. Each node can:
• Store a copy of the ledger

g.data-narration: Hash functions, digital signatures, and Merkle trees secure blockchain data.
Smart contracts are self-executing programs that automatically enforce agreements on blockchain.
g.text: Smart contracts are self-executing programs stored on blockchain that automatically
enforce agreements when predefined conditions are met. They eliminate intermediaries.

g.data-narration: DApps are decentralized applications built on blockchain with no central authority.
g.text: DApps are applications built on blockchain that operate without central authority:

g.data-narration: DAO, DAC, and DAS represent different governance models for decentralized organizations.
Popular blockchain platforms include Ethereum, Hyperledger Fabric, and high-TPS alternatives.
Decentralization offers no single point of failure, transparency, security, and lower costs.
Real-world applications include DeFi, NFTs, supply chain, identity management, and voting.
Key takeaways: blockchain enables decentralization through consensus, P2P, and cryptography.
g.text: • Blockchain enables decentralization through consensus, P2P networks, and cryptography
• Smart contracts provide trustless execution of agreements
• DAO/DAC/DAS represent different governance models for decentralized systems

g.data-narration: Decentralization represents the future of trustless and transparent digital systems.
g.text: Decentralization is the Future of Trustless, Transparent Systems
